chief_complaint: A 30-year-old pregnant woman in the early stage of pregnancy presented with fever and cough.
history: The patient was initially diagnosed with pneumonia and treated with penicillin, but her symptoms persisted. She was then switched to cephalosporin antibiotics combined with methylprednisolone, which provided temporary relief. However, her symptoms recurred after discontinuation of the medication.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 38.5°C, blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, pulse 100 beats per minute. Physical examination revealed bilateral lung crackles.
labs: Chest contrast-enhanced computed tomography confirmed pulmonary embolism. ECG showed no significant abnormalities. Blood tests revealed elevated D-dimer levels.
